runtime.boundary: official Python Transformers and vLLM backends; streaming is vLLM-only

energy.notes: The smaller 0.6B model was measured first through the reference-only MLX path.

engineering_cost.conversion_effort: The architecture work is shared with Qwen3-ASR 0.6B, but the product path still requires a repository-owned C++ implementation over official MLX.
engineering_cost.update_cost: Official Qwen changes and model conversion would be tracked by the same owned adapter proposed for the 0.6B candidate.
engineering_cost.packaging_cost: The official BF16 artifact is about 4.70 GB of repository storage before an MLX quantized conversion and is substantially larger than the measured 0.6B reference.

reproduction.command: No product benchmark command: measure the smaller 0.6B reference first, then port the shared architecture over official MLX before considering the larger weights.

result.blocker: The official runtime is Python through Transformers or vLLM; streaming is vLLM-only and timestamps require a separate 0.6B forced aligner. With no accepted Apple-local runtime, the 1.7B weights add size before resolving the same interop boundary already exposed by the measured 0.6B model.
observations: Qwen3-ASR 0.6B was measured through a pinned reference-only MLX port and already crossed the quality threshold.
The official streaming path does not return timestamps.
The larger model should be reconsidered only if an owned 0.6B official-MLX port shows a product-quality gap.
